body: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) and World Bank have projected Indonesia's economic growth to be 5.1% in 2025, lower than the government's expectations, citing weak household consumption, particularly among lower-middle-income groups. The global economy is expected to remain stable at 3.3% according to the IMF, and 2.7% according to the World Bank, with the Indonesian government remaining optimistic despite the lowered forecasts.
title: IMF and World Bank Lower Indonesia's Economic Growth Forecast to 5.1 Percent

body: PT Raharja Energi Cepu TBK led the top gainers list with a 142.15% price increase, followed by PT Solusi Sinergi Digital TBK with a 142.11% rise, and PT Citra Marga Nusaphala Persada TBK with a 95.39% increase. Other notable gainers included PT Era Media Sejahtera TBK, PT Jakarta Setiabudi Internasional TBK, PT Daaz Bara Lestari TBK, PT Super Energi TBK, PT Sinergi Inti Andalan Prima TBK, PT Sau Visi Putra TBK, and PT Remala Abadi TBK, with price increases ranging from 28.10% to 63.16%.
title: Jakarta Stocks See Significant Gains with PT Raharja Energi Cepu Leading the Pack

body: The Jakarta Composite Index rose 0.93% to 7,154.66, driven by gains in banking and property sectors, with PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI) contributing 24.57 index points and PT Dian Swastatika Sentosa Tbk (DSSA) adding 18.22 points. Meanwhile, foreign investors recorded a net buy of IDR 247.45 billion, a significant reversal from the previous week's net sell of IDR 2.12 trillion, as transaction value increased 33.50% to IDR 11.64 trillion.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Rises 0.93% to 7,154.66, Led by Banking and Property Sectors.

body: The planned merger between PT XL Axiata Tbk (EXCL) and PT Smartfren Telecom Tbk has not yet received full approval from creditors, with one vendor submitting an objection letter on December 24, 2024. The companies are required to obtain creditor approval to proceed with the merger, which is expected to be completed in the first half of 2025, pending resolution of the objection.
title: XL Axiata and Smartfren's planned merger delayed due to pending creditor approval.

body: Indonesia's motor vehicle market has shown signs of recovery, with electric vehicle sales surging 170% year-on-year to 27,500 units, while four-wheeled vehicle wholesale sales decreased 16% to 633,000 units and two-wheeled vehicle sales rose 3.3% to 4.3 million units. PT Astra International Tbk (ASII) continues to dominate the low-cost green car and electric vehicle segments, controlling 80% of the market share, and Ina Sekuritas recommends ASII and PT Dharma Polimetal Tbk stocks with target prices of IDR 1,120 and IDR 6,175, respectively.
title: Indonesian Motor Vehicle Market Shows Recovery Signs Despite Challenges Ahead

body: PT Sumber Aneka Gas (SURE) has secured a natural gas allocation of 71.01 billion standard cubic feet from the Sumber gas field in Tuban, with a gas sales agreement signed with PT Pertamina Hulu Energi (PHE-TEJ) that will run until December 31, 2036. The new gas supply is expected to have a positive impact on the company's finances, improving its financial position, and will ensure a stable gas supply for its consumers.
title: PT Sumber Aneka Gas secures 71.01 billion standard cubic feet of natural gas allocation from Sumber gas field in Tuban.

body: Investment realisation in Indonesia is expected to grow 8.83% in Q1 2025, albeit at a slower pace compared to Q4 2024's 9.59% growth, driven by sectors such as agriculture, wholesale retail trade, and transportation-warehousing. According to Bank Indonesia's business activity survey, 24.79% of respondents plan to invest in the first half of 2025, citing obstacles such as licensing, taxation, and interest rates.
title: Jakarta's investment realisation in Q1 2025 expected to grow at slower pace than Q4 2024.

body: PT Darma Henwa Tbk (DEWA) will convert IDR 1.11 trillion of debt to two creditors, PT Madhani Talatah Nusantara (MTN) and PT Andhesti Tungkas Pratama (ATP), by issuing 17.16 billion new shares at IDR 65 each, resulting in a significant change to DEWA's shareholder structure. Post-conversion, DEWA's authorized capital will increase to IDR 9.81 trillion, and its shareholder structure will include MTN with 29.84%, ATP with 14.15%, and public shareholders with 39.79%.
title: PT Darma Henwa to Settle IDR 1.11 Trillion Debt by Issuing New Shares to Creditors

body: PT Medco Power Indonesia (no ticker) will issue a shelf-registration sukuk wakalah worth IDR 1.15 trillion to repay several maturing bonds and bank loans, including IDR 258 billion and IDR 43 billion bonds maturing on July 4, 2025, and IDR 280 billion sukuk wakalah maturing on August 4, 2025. The sukuk wakalah will be offered in three series with yields ranging from 7.75% to 9.50% per year, with underwriters including PT BNI Sekuritas, PT BRI Danareksa Sekuritas, PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk (TRIM), and PT Bank Mega Tbk (BMRI).
title: Medco Power Indonesia to Issue IDR 1.15 Trillion Sukuk Wakalah to Repay Maturing Bonds and Loans.
